key_themes: Strong FY2025 earnings with €2.1bn profit after tax and 25% RoTE
Generous capital returns including 58% higher dividend and €1bn share buyback
Full privatization and complete Irish State exit removing overhang
Multiple analyst upgrades with raised price targets (Citi, Deutsche Bank, JPM)
High dividend yield (~6-9%) and attractive valuation with strong long-term performance
summary: AIB Group (AIBG.L / A5G.IR), the major Irish bank, reported robust FY2025 results in March 2026 with €2.1 billion profit after tax, 25% return on tangible equity, strong deposit growth, and significant green lending. This performance supported a 58% increase in the annual dividend, a €1 billion share buyback, and marked the full exit of the Irish government's stake, returning the bank to complete private ownership. Recent analyst upgrades, raised price targets, solid 2026 guidance (RoTE >20%, modest loan growth), and strong multi-year share price gains (+85% 1yr) drive clearly positive sentiment, despite expected near-term pressure on net interest income from lower rates.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AIBG.L/)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/AIBG.I/)[[3]](https://aib.ie/investorrelations/financial-information/results-centre/2025-financial-results)
sources: 10+ news articles and filings (Yahoo Finance, Reuters, AIB investor site, TipRanks, GuruFocus), Analyst notes from Citi, Deutsche Bank, JPM, Barclays, ~8 relevant recent X posts (earnings, upgrades, broker targets), Investor discussion forums (ADVFN, Investing.com, LSE share chat)
